### Post by llamakc on 2007-06-24
That's because you need to tell it to load metacity.

Start up again (not failsafe).

Hit ctrl-F2 and type

metacity --replace

---

### Post by Sushubh on 2007-06-24
ok that worked. but after restarting again... i was back with no window title bars! 

how to make metacity default?

---

### Post by gkiller on 2007-06-24
> **Sushubh said:**
> ok that worked. but after restarting again... i was back with no window title bars! 

how to make metacity default?
Go into the menu System > Preferences > Sessions
Then remove your compiz/beryl entry, and add a new one with "metacity --replace".

---

### Post by Sushubh on 2007-06-25
could not find an entry for compiz fusion... but adding metacity works.

---

### Post by Sushubh on 2007-06-25
ok its weird.

when i boot in (without metacity --enable) i see no titlebars. 

with metacity i see regular windows no effects. 

with compiz --replace compiz works and with titlebars! 

so i guess the entries into the conf file fixed the issues with compiz... but something messed up during the bootup process.

### Post by gkiller on 2007-06-27
> **shinjo101788 said:**
> everything seems to work except when i colose the terminal it gors back to normal. and by normal i mean no borders and the nautilus thingy on my desktop. When you close the terminal, every program started within it will be terminated. To start compiz without having to leave the terminal open, press Alt-F2 and type your command there.

### Post by rishabh on 2007-06-27
Install Emerald theme manager:

```
sudo apt-get install emerald emerald-themes
```

To run Compiz, type 

```
compiz --replace -c emerald &
```

Correct me if I'm wrong...

---

### Post by shinjo101788 on 2007-06-27
it still doesnt work. i typed it in in the terminal and in the altF2 window. restarted and no dice.  the nautilius thing is still up and i  have no window borders.

---

### Post by rishabh on 2007-06-27
Type

```
sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```


and add the following at the end:

```
Section "Extensions"
    Option         "Composite" "Enable"
EndSection
```

---

### Post by shinjo101788 on 2007-06-27
now my xserver syas i have no screens found and i cant even have a gui anymore.  Is there a command to edit the text file from the command line?

---

### Post by rishabh on 2007-06-28
Certainly, there is.
Use the command line editor "nano"

```
sudo nano /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

And you could try reconfiguring the xserver by

```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```

---

### Post by Sushubh on 2007-06-28
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg does not seem to detect the graphic card for me. would i required to reinstall nvidia thing from automatix?

---

### Post by rishabh on 2007-06-28
@Sushubh:
I don't see why that should happen...
If you havn't installed the nVidia drivers, you should go to Administration->Restricted Drivers Manager and enable nvidia (on Feisty). If your GUI doesnt work at all, then use

```
sudo apt-get install nvidia-glx
```

With the advent of Feisty, it's been quite some time since I bothered about display drivers :p

---

### Post by Sushubh on 2007-06-28
cool thanks!
